component_identification.content: Core components include 19 terrain hexes, number tokens, resource cards (brick, lumber, wool, grain, ore), development cards, roads/settlements/cities in four player colors, a robber pawn, two dice, ports, and bonus cards for Longest Road and Largest Army.

core_game_loop.content: Each turn you roll dice to produce resources, resolve robber effects on a 7, optionally trade with players or bank/ports, then spend resources to build roads, settlements, cities, or buy a development card.

detailed_rules.content: Setup uses randomized terrain and number tokens (with spacing rules) and two starting settlements per player. Settlements must follow the distance rule (no adjacent settlements). Roads extend your network and enable new settlement spots. Cities upgrade settlements and double production from adjacent hexes. On a 7, players with more than seven cards discard half and the active player moves the robber to block one hex and steal one random card from an adjacent opponent. Development cards are bought face down; most cannot be played the same turn they are purchased. First to a continuous 5-road chain claims Longest Road (+2 VP), and first to three played Knights claims Largest Army (+2 VP).

scoring_and_endgame.content: Points come from settlements (1), cities (2), certain development cards (1 each), Longest Road (2), and Largest Army (2). The game ends immediately when a player reaches 10+ points on their own turn.

beginner_strategy.content: Prioritize strong starting intersections with high-probability numbers (6/8) and balanced resource access. Expand early with road+settlement tempo, protect against robber risk by spending cards before turns cycle, and use ports or smart trades to smooth bottlenecks. Track opponents near 10 VP and contest Longest Road/Largest Army when possible.

metadata.notes: Check 4 failed: total_token_count is 263; must be 800-3000 (hard reject if <800 or >5000)
